papers.title: King's Health Partners Prostate Cancer BioBank (KHP PCaBB): design and initial cohort
papers.institution: King's Health Partners Prostate Cancer BioBank (KHP PCaBB) / King's College London
papers.access_route: Application to KHP PCaBB access committee (prostatecancerbiobank.org.uk); IRB and MTA required
papers.notes: n=489 serum at -80C + n=1064 FFPE; Gleason score documented on all cases; acinar adenocarcinoma confirmed histologically; radical prostatectomy and biopsy specimens; PSA trajectory documented; London-based UK collection

papers.title: Prostate Cancer Biorepository Network (PCBN): a DoD-funded multi-site biobank
papers.institution: Prostate Cancer Biorepository Network (PCBN) / WashU / DoD
papers.access_route: Application to PCBN; DoD-funded network; coordinated through WashU; contact pcbn@wustl.edu
papers.notes: FFPE + serum from radical prostatectomy; Gleason documented; multi-site US network; DoD-funded; acinar adenocarcinoma focus; pathology review centralized at WashU; access via application

papers.title: Weill Cornell Medicine SPORE in Prostate Cancer: biobank and clinical resource
papers.institution: Weill Cornell Medicine SPORE / Englander Institute for Precision Medicine
papers.access_route: WCM SPORE collaborative access; NCI SPORE program; IRB and MTA required; contact SPORE administrative core
papers.notes: Radical prostatectomy biobank with FFPE, plasma, serum, and urine; Gleason and ISUP grade group documented; acinar adenocarcinoma confirmed; genomic data (WGS, RNA-seq) linked on subset; longitudinal plasma at recurrence

expected_retrievable_institutions: King's Health Partners Prostate Cancer BioBank (KHP PCaBB)
notes: KHP PCaBB (UK) has the clearest access route via a dedicated portal and n=489 serum + n=1064 FFPE. PCBN is the US multi-site equivalent via DoD with acinar adenocarcinoma focus. WCM SPORE adds genomic linkage and longitudinal plasma. All three require formal application with IRB.
